title: Talk:Wikipedia and the Israeli–Palestinian conflict

The Tool They’re Using Is Horrifying. |date3 = February 5, 2025 |org3 = [[Slate (magazine)|Slate]] |url3 = https://slate.com/technology/2025/02/wikipedia-project-2025-heritage-foundation-doxing-editors-antisemitism.html |lang3 = |quote3 = Let’s be clear: Wikipedia’s handling of this topic area is incredibly contentious. Many Wikipedians deliberately avoid pages like “Gaza War,” “Zionism,” and even the meta-entry on Wikipedia’s own coverage of the Israeli–Palestinian conflict. |archiveurl3 = |archivedate3 = |accessdate3 = February 5, 2025 |author4 = Jon Brodkin |title4 = Ted Cruz doesn’t seem to understand Wikipedia, lawyer for Wikimedia says |date4 = October 7, 2025 |org4 = [[Ars Technica]] |url4 = https://arstechnica.com/tech-policy/2025/10/wikipedia-rebuts-ted-cruz-attack-says-cruz-just-doesnt-understand-the-site/ |lang4 = |quote4 = He described the Wikimedia Foundation as "intervening in editorial decisions" in an apparent reference to an incident in which the platform's Arbitration Committee responded to editing conflicts on the Israeli–Palestinian conflict by banning eight editors. |archiveurl4 = |archivedate4 = |accessdate4 = October 9, 2025 }} {{User:ClueBot III/ArchiveThis | age =2160 | archiveprefix =Talk:Wikipedia and the Israeli–Palestinian conflict/Archive | numberstart =1 | maxarchsize =75000 | header ={{Archive}} | minkeepthreads =5 | format = %%i }}{{Archives|bot=ClueBot III|age=90}} == ref == Not sure if anything here is helpful for this article: * https://www.theverge.com/cs/features/717322/wikipedia-attacks-neutrality-history-jimmy-wales --[[User:Another Believer|Another Believer]] ([[User talk:Another Believer|Talk]]) 18:34, 4 September 2025 (UTC) :cool article. might be worth posting somewhere more central, it seems to touch multiple aspects of wikipedia. [[User:Bluethricecreamman|Bluethricecreamman]] ([[User talk:Bluethricecreamman|talk]]) 18:56, 4 September 2025 (UTC) ::"''[https://www.theverge.com/cs/features/717322/wikipedia-attacks-neutrality-history-jimmy-wales Consensus was reached. The lines were added back. Approximately 7,000 words of deliberation to settle, for a time, three sentences. This was Wikipedia’s process working as intended.]''" Do we love this place despite that or because of it? And the ending was just brilliant. Feel free to add on more pages. [[User:Gråbergs Gråa Sång|Gråbergs Gråa Sång]] ([[User talk:Gråbergs Gråa Sång|talk]]) 21:01, 4 September 2025 (UTC) == Article is spiking a little == [https://pageviews.wmcloud.org/?project=en.wikipedia.org&platform=all-access&agent=user&redirects=0&range=latest-20&pages=Wikipedia_and_the_Israeli%E2%80%93Palestinian_conflict] Any guesses where that came from? [[User:Gråbergs Gråa Sång|Gråbergs Gråa Sång]] ([[User talk:Gråbergs Gråa Sång|talk]]) 08:31, 20 October 2025 (UTC) == Recent news-coverage on Jimmy Wales and [[Gaza Genocide]] == [[WP:GIZMODO]] wrote this: "[https://gizmodo.com/wikipedia-gaza-genocide-locked-2000680991 Wikipedia co-founder Jimmy Wales made the extraordinary move Sunday to lock down the online encyclopedia’s English-language entry on the genocide in Gaza. Nobody can edit the text of the article.]" However, you can see at [https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Gaza_genocide&action=history] that this is wrong. If the writer copied [[WP:NYPOST]] or didn't watch for AI hallucinations I don't know, but it wasn't Wales, it wasn't Sunday, and it's disappointing [[WP:GIZMODO]] can't describe [[WP:GOLDLOCK]] better. [[User:Gråbergs Gråa Sång|Gråbergs Gråa Sång]] ([[User talk:Gråbergs Gråa Sång|talk]]) 14:32, 4 November 2025 (UTC) :To their credit, they have now updated: "Wikipedia co-founder Jimmy Wales commented on the online encyclopedia’s English-language entry about the genocide in Gaza on Sunday, which had been previously locked over disputes about its neutrality." [[User:Gråbergs Gråa Sång|Gråbergs Gråa Sång]] ([[User talk:Gråbergs Gråa Sång|talk]]) 15:12, 4 November 2025 (UTC) :Aljazeera made the correction too:[https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2025/11/4/why-did-wikipedia-cofounder-block-edits-to-the-gaza-genocide-page] [[User:Gråbergs Gråa Sång|Gråbergs Gråa Sång]] ([[User talk:Gråbergs Gråa Sång|talk]]) 06:31, 5 November 2025 (UTC) == Images added and removed == I added a couple of images at "[https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Wikipedia_and_the_Israeli%E2%80%93Palestinian_conflict&diff=prev&oldid=1320695064 images of 2014 Gaza War, captions copypasted from 2014 Gaza War, illustrating the "In 2014, Euronews reported..." paragraph]", they were removed at "[https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Wikipedia_and_the_Israeli%E2%80%93Palestinian_conflict&diff=prev&oldid=1321600764 -images not particularly helpful]". They're the leadimages from [[2014 Gaza War]], I think they fit pretty well, but there's certainly others to chose from. Opinions, editors? [[User:Gråbergs Gråa Sång|Gråbergs Gråa Sång]] ([[User talk:Gråbergs Gråa Sång|talk]]) 05:44, 12 November 2025 (UTC) == The Wales files == @[[User:1timeuse75|1timeuse75]] What do you think of this [https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Wikipedia_and_the_Israeli%E2%80%93Palestinian_conflict&diff=prev&oldid=1336192367] "solution?" [[User:Gråbergs Gråa Sång|Gråbergs Gråa Sång]] ([[User talk:Gråbergs Gråa Sång|talk]]) 11:51, 2 February 2026 (UTC) :@[[User:Gråbergs Gråa Sång|Gråbergs Gråa Sång]] Remember to remove [[Underscore|underscores]] from links. I think it works IMO [[User:1timeuse75|1timeuse75]] ([[User talk:1timeuse75|talk]]) 12:00, 2 February 2026 (UTC) ::It's unusual, but the thing had some media coverage and may be of interest to readers. We'll see what happens. [[User:Gråbergs Gråa Sång|Gråbergs Gråa Sång]] ([[User talk:Gråbergs Gråa Sång|talk]]) 12:03, 2 February 2026 (UTC) :::But this link does comply with [[MOS:SELFREF]]. [[User:Absolutiva|Absolutiva]] 13:12, 19 February 2026 (UTC) ::::@[[User:Absolutiva|Absolutiva]] Nothing at [[MOS:SELFREF]] comments on your removal here:[https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Wikipedia_and_the_Israeli%E2%80%93Palestinian_conflict&curid=75611836&diff=1339202789&oldid=1336283800]. A See also link is not a reference. Consider also that the discussion is mentioned in the article. That said, it's a local consensus thing. And I'm guessing you meant "doesn't comply". [[User:Gråbergs Gråa Sång|Gråbergs Gråa Sång]] ([[User talk:Gråbergs Gråa Sång|talk]]) 13:12, 19 February 2026 (UTC) :::::Wait, what should we place into {{tl|srlink}} instead? [[User:Absolutiva|Absolutiva]] 13:45, 19 February 2026 (UTC) ::::::You mean putting ::::::*{{Srlink|Talk:Gaza genocide/Archive_22#Statement from Jimbo Wales|Statement from Jimbo Wales}}, November 2025 discussion on the [[Gaza genocide]] talkpage, including comments by [[Jimmy Wales]] and [[Larry Sanger]] ::::::under See also instead? I see no big difference, but if that makes you happier, sure. [[User:Gråbergs Gråa Sång|Gråbergs Gråa Sång]] ([[User talk:Gråbergs Gråa Sång|talk]]) 13:56, 19 February 2026 (UTC)
